messages.content:
{
  "phase": "questions",
  "section": "project_context",
  "questions": [
    "What is the current delivery scope and timeline (e.g., PoC for Screening Room now vs broader MVP), and which modules are in scope for this phase?",
    "What are the top 3 risk areas or failure modes you’re most concerned about for Screening Room (e.g., video upload/playback reliability, timecode accuracy, comment locking on completion)?",
    "How is the team structured for this phase (number of developers, any dedicated QA), and who owns test reviews and sign-off?",
    "What is your planned release cadence and environment setup (dev/staging/production), including any constraints on test data in shared environments?"
  ],
  "summary": "We’re initiating a test strategy for the Rivvy Portal with an immediate focus on the Screening Room epic (single-project scope, comments tied to latest version, producer-controlled completion lock). We need clarity on delivery scope/timeline, key risks to prioritize, team/QA ownership, and environment/release cadence to shape the test approach."
}

questions: What is the current delivery scope and timeline (e.g., PoC for Screening Room now vs broader MVP), and which modules are in scope for this phase?
What are the top 3 risk areas or failure modes you’re most concerned about for Screening Room (e.g., video upload/playback reliability, timecode accuracy, comment locking on completion)?
How is the team structured for this phase (number of developers, any dedicated QA), and who owns test reviews and sign-off?
What is your planned release cadence and environment setup (dev/staging/production), including any constraints on test data in shared environments?
structuredResponse.summary: We’re initiating a test strategy for the Rivvy Portal with an immediate focus on the Screening Room epic (single-project scope, comments tied to latest version, producer-controlled completion lock). We need clarity on delivery scope/timeline, key risks to prioritize, team/QA ownership, and environment/release cadence to shape the test approach.

messages.content:
1. Q: What is the current delivery scope and timeline (e.g., PoC for Screening Room now vs broader MVP), and which modules are in scope for this phase? A: PoC in March 2026, MVP in May 2026
2. Q: What are the top 3 risk areas or failure modes you’re most concerned about for Screening Room (e.g., video upload/playback reliability, timecode accuracy, comment locking on completion)? A: Yes, those 3
3. Q: How is the team structured for this phase (number of developers, any dedicated QA), and who owns test reviews and sign-off? A: Single engineer to build and test, no dedicated QAs
4. Q: What is your planned release cadence and environment setup (dev/staging/production), including any constraints on test data in shared environments? A: Ad-hoc for now

{
  "phase": "questions",
  "section": "test_levels",
  "questions": [
    "For the PoC vs MVP, which test levels are must-have for PoC: unit (frontend/backend), integration (API+DB), end-to-end browser flows, and any minimal performance/security/accessibility checks?",
    "Do you have a preference for end-to-end tooling (Playwright vs Cypress) for Screening Room happy paths, or should we proceed with Playwright for speed and cross-browser later?",
    "Can we use Testcontainers with PostgreSQL for backend integration tests in CI, or do you prefer an in-memory or shared DB for now?",
    "Where will video files be stored in the PoC (local filesystem, S3, other), and should tests hit a real bucket/emulator or use mocks/stubs?"
  ],
  "summary": "Context captured: PoC targeted for March 2026 and MVP in May 2026. Top risks to prioritize are video upload/playback reliability, timecode accuracy, and strict comment locking when marked complete. A single engineer will build and test with ad-hoc releases. Next, we need to lock in test levels for PoC (unit/integration/E2E emphasis), choose an E2E tool, confirm DB integration approach (e.g., Testcontainers), and clarify video storage to shape integration/E2E scope."
}

questions: For the PoC vs MVP, which test levels are must-have for PoC: unit (frontend/backend), integration (API+DB), end-to-end browser flows, and any minimal performance/security/accessibility checks?
Do you have a preference for end-to-end tooling (Playwright vs Cypress) for Screening Room happy paths, or should we proceed with Playwright for speed and cross-browser later?
Can we use Testcontainers with PostgreSQL for backend integration tests in CI, or do you prefer an in-memory or shared DB for now?
Where will video files be stored in the PoC (local filesystem, S3, other), and should tests hit a real bucket/emulator or use mocks/stubs?
structuredResponse.summary: Context captured: PoC targeted for March 2026 and MVP in May 2026. Top risks to prioritize are video upload/playback reliability, timecode accuracy, and strict comment locking when marked complete. A single engineer will build and test with ad-hoc releases. Next, we need to lock in test levels for PoC (unit/integration/E2E emphasis), choose an E2E tool, confirm DB integration approach (e.g., Testcontainers), and clarify video storage to shape integration/E2E scope.

messages.content:
1. Q: For the PoC vs MVP, which test levels are must-have for PoC: unit (frontend/backend), integration (API+DB), end-to-end browser flows, and any minimal performance/security/accessibility checks? A: unit (frontend/backend), functional, integration (API+DB), end-to-end browser flows.  Light touch NFR related tests for MVP only
2. Q: Do you have a preference for end-to-end tooling (Playwright vs Cypress) for Screening Room happy paths, or should we proceed with Playwright for speed and cross-browser later? A: Playwright
3. Q: Can we use Testcontainers with PostgreSQL for backend integration tests in CI, or do you prefer an in-memory or shared DB for now? A: Testcontainers with PostgreSQL for backend integration tests in CI
4. Q: Where will video files be stored in the PoC (local filesystem, S3, other), and should tests hit a real bucket/emulator or use mocks/stubs? A: S3 and tests hit a real bucket

{
  "phase": "questions",
  "section": "coverage_targets",
  "questions": [
    "Approve these PoC CI coverage thresholds: Frontend unit ≥75% lines and ≥60% branches; Backend unit ≥80% lines and ≥70% branches. Adjust if needed.",
    "Integration/E2E coverage proposal: Integration tests exercise all DB-touching repositories/services (happy + at least one failure path each); E2E covers these critical flows — (1) Producer uploads a new version; (2) Client comments on latest version and clicking a timecoded comment seeks playback; (3) Viewing a non-latest version prevents new comments and forces new comments to latest; (4) Producer marks complete, which locks comments/uploads, and can be reversed. OK?",
    "Mutation testing: adopt for MVP only (targets: Backend PIT ≥60% mutation score; Frontend Stryker optional), and skip for PoC. Agree?",
    "Confirm that performance, security, and accessibility coverage targets are excluded from PoC and deferred as light-touch checks for MVP."
  ],
  "summary": "Test levels set for PoC: frontend/backend unit, functional, integration (API+PostgreSQL via Testcontainers), and Playwright E2E; NFR-style tests deferred to MVP. Video storage via S3 with tests using a real bucket. Next, we need to lock CI coverage thresholds for unit tests, define integration/E2E critical-path coverage expectations, decide on mutation testing timing/targets, and confirm exclusion of NFR coverage from the PoC."
}

questions: Approve these PoC CI coverage thresholds: Frontend unit ≥75% lines and ≥60% branches; Backend unit ≥80% lines and ≥70% branches. Adjust if needed.

Integration/E2E coverage proposal: Integration tests exercise all DB-touching repositories/services (happy + at least one failure path each); E2E covers these critical flows — (1) Producer uploads a new version; (2) Client comments on latest version and clicking a timecoded comment seeks playback; (3) Viewing a non-latest version prevents new comments and forces new comments to latest; (4) Producer marks complete, which locks comments/uploads, and can be reversed. OK?
Mutation testing: adopt for MVP only (targets: Backend PIT ≥60% mutation score; Frontend Stryker optional), and skip for PoC. Agree?
Confirm that performance, security, and accessibility coverage targets are excluded from PoC and deferred as light-touch checks for MVP.
structuredResponse.summary: Test levels set for PoC: frontend/backend unit, functional, integration (API+PostgreSQL via Testcontainers), and Playwright E2E; NFR-style tests deferred to MVP. Video storage via S3 with tests using a real bucket. Next, we need to lock CI coverage thresholds for unit tests, define integration/E2E critical-path coverage expectations, decide on mutation testing timing/targets, and confirm exclusion of NFR coverage from the PoC.

messages.content:
1. Q: Approve these PoC CI coverage thresholds: Frontend unit ≥75% lines and ≥60% branches; Backend unit ≥80% lines and ≥70% branches. Adjust if needed. A: Approve
2. Q: Integration/E2E coverage proposal: Integration tests exercise all DB-touching repositories/services (happy + at least one failure path each); E2E covers these critical flows — (1) Producer uploads a new version; (2) Client comments on latest version and clicking a timecoded comment seeks playback; (3) Viewing a non-latest version prevents new comments and forces new comments to latest; (4) Producer marks complete, which locks comments/uploads, and can be reversed. OK? A: Ok
3. Q: Mutation testing: adopt for MVP only (targets: Backend PIT ≥60% mutation score; Frontend Stryker optional), and skip for PoC. Agree? A: Agree
4. Q: Confirm that performance, security, and accessibility coverage targets are excluded from PoC and deferred as light-touch checks for MVP. A: Confirmed

{
  "phase": "questions",
  "section": "tooling",
  "questions": [
    "Frontend: Confirm Vitest + React Testing Library (+ @testing-library/user-event), MSW for API mocking, c8/Istanbul for coverage, and ESLint + Prettier + TypeScript checks via pre-commit (Husky/lint-staged). OK?",
    "Backend: Confirm JUnit 5 + Spring Boot Test, Testcontainers (PostgreSQL), Mockito for mocking, JaCoCo for coverage in CI, and PIT mutation testing starting in MVP. OK?",
    "E2E (Playwright): Should PoC run Chromium-only in CI with trace/video/screenshots on failure, and expand to cross-browser in MVP?",
    "Auth & Data for E2E: Use real login flow with seeded test users or a test-only auth bypass? How should test data be seeded/reset (API seeding endpoints vs DB reset/fixtures)?",
    "S3 in CI: Do we have a dedicated test bucket and IAM creds for CI with a bucket prefix (e.g., e2e/{buildId}/) and a life-cycle rule (e.g., auto-delete after 7 days)?"
  ],
  "summary": "PoC scope: unit (FE/BE), functional, integration (API+Postgres via Testcontainers), and Playwright E2E. Approved unit coverage targets (FE 75/60, BE 80/70). Integration/E2E must cover critical Screening Room flows including upload, timecoded comments on latest version, non-latest read-only with forced-latest commenting, and reversible completion lock. Mutation testing deferred to MVP; NFR checks (perf/sec/a11y) also deferred. Next we need to confirm concrete tooling stacks for FE/BE/E2E, decide E2E authentication and data seeding/reset approach, and ensure S3 CI access and retention controls."
}

questions: Frontend: Confirm Vitest + React Testing Library (+ @testing-library/user-event), MSW for API mocking, c8/Istanbul for coverage, and ESLint + Prettier + TypeScript checks via pre-commit (Husky/lint-staged). OK?
Backend: Confirm JUnit 5 + Spring Boot Test, Testcontainers (PostgreSQL), Mockito for mocking, JaCoCo for coverage in CI, and PIT mutation testing starting in MVP. OK?
E2E (Playwright): Should PoC run Chromium-only in CI with trace/video/screenshots on failure, and expand to cross-browser in MVP?
Auth & Data for E2E: Use real login flow with seeded test users or a test-only auth bypass? How should test data be seeded/reset (API seeding endpoints vs DB reset/fixtures)?
S3 in CI: Do we have a dedicated test bucket and IAM creds for CI with a bucket prefix (e.g., e2e/{buildId}/) and a life-cycle rule (e.g., auto-delete after 7 days)?
structuredResponse.summary: PoC scope: unit (FE/BE), functional, integration (API+Postgres via Testcontainers), and Playwright E2E. Approved unit coverage targets (FE 75/60, BE 80/70). Integration/E2E must cover critical Screening Room flows including upload, timecoded comments on latest version, non-latest read-only with forced-latest commenting, and reversible completion lock. Mutation testing deferred to MVP; NFR checks (perf/sec/a11y) also deferred. Next we need to confirm concrete tooling stacks for FE/BE/E2E, decide E2E authentication and data seeding/reset approach, and ensure S3 CI access and retention controls.

messages.content:
1. Q: Frontend: Confirm Vitest + React Testing Library (+ @testing-library/user-event), MSW for API mocking, c8/Istanbul for coverage, and ESLint + Prettier + TypeScript checks via pre-commit (Husky/lint-staged). OK? A: Ok
2. Q: Backend: Confirm JUnit 5 + Spring Boot Test, Testcontainers (PostgreSQL), Mockito for mocking, JaCoCo for coverage in CI, and PIT mutation testing starting in MVP. OK? A: Ok
3. Q: E2E (Playwright): Should PoC run Chromium-only in CI with trace/video/screenshots on failure, and expand to cross-browser in MVP? A: Chromium-only
4. Q: Auth & Data for E2E: Use real login flow with seeded test users or a test-only auth bypass? How should test data be seeded/reset (API seeding endpoints vs DB reset/fixtures)? A: Use real login flow with seeded test users.  Potential seed data is added using liquibase
5. Q: S3 in CI: Do we have a dedicated test bucket and IAM creds for CI with a bucket prefix (e.g., e2e/{buildId}/) and a life-cycle rule (e.g., auto-delete after 7 days)? A: We can create them as necessary

{
  "phase": "questions",
  "section": "quality_gates",
  "questions": [
    "PR gates: Approve requiring lint, typecheck, frontend/backend unit tests with the approved coverage thresholds, and backend integration tests to pass on every PR; allow self-approval (single engineer) but PR required for all changes?",
    "E2E execution: Run Playwright (Chromium-only) on every PR against a temporary preview/staging environment, or only on merges to main? Which environment should E2E target (dedicated staging vs ephemeral per-PR)?",
    "Pre-deployment checks: Confirm mandatory Liquibase validate/migrate, a short smoke suite (upload, comment seek, complete/uncomplete), S3 write/read smoke, and rollback plan (e.g., DB backup + app rollback). OK?",
    "Security gates for PoC: enable basic dependency and secret scanning in CI (e.g., OWASP/Snyk and git-secrets) now, or defer these to MVP?"
  ],
  "summary": "Tooling confirmed: FE Vitest + RTL + MSW with c8; BE JUnit5 + Spring Boot Test + Mockito + Testcontainers + JaCoCo; Playwright E2E (Chromium only in CI); real login with seeded users (Liquibase), S3 real bucket in CI (to be created with lifecycle/cleanup). Coverage targets approved (FE 75/60, BE 80/70). Integration/E2E must cover upload, timecoded comments on latest, non-latest read-only with forced-latest commenting, and reversible completion lock. Next, we need to formalize PR and CI quality gates, decide when/where E2E runs, define pre-deployment validations and rollback, and choose whether to include minimal security scanning for the PoC."
}

Coverage targets approved (FE 75/60, BE 80/70). Integration/E2E must cover upload, timecoded comments on latest, non-latest read-only with forced-latest commenting, and reversible completion lock. Next, we need to formalize PR and CI quality gates, decide when/where E2E runs, define pre-deployment validations and rollback, and choose whether to include minimal security scanning for the PoC.

messages.content:
1. Q: PR gates: Approve requiring lint, typecheck, frontend/backend unit tests with the approved coverage thresholds, and backend integration tests to pass on every PR; allow self-approval (single engineer) but PR required for all changes? A: Yes
2. Q: E2E execution: Run Playwright (Chromium-only) on every PR against a temporary preview/staging environment, or only on merges to main? Which environment should E2E target (dedicated staging vs ephemeral per-PR)? A: only on merges to main
3. Q: Pre-deployment checks: Confirm mandatory Liquibase validate/migrate, a short smoke suite (upload, comment seek, complete/uncomplete), S3 write/read smoke, and rollback plan (e.g., DB backup + app rollback). OK? A: Ok
4. Q: Security gates for PoC: enable basic dependency and secret scanning in CI (e.g., OWASP/Snyk and git-secrets) now, or defer these to MVP? A: defer these to MVP

{
  "phase": "questions",
  "section": "testing_principles",
  "questions": [
    "Development approach: adopt test-first for unit/integration on risky logic, with test-after acceptable for UI wiring. Approve?",
    "Flaky tests policy: allow 1 retry for E2E; if still failing, fail the pipeline. Quarantine only with an issue opened and fix within 48 hours. Approve?",
    "E2E data isolation: suite creates its own project/entities, uses a unique S3 key prefix per run, and cleans up at end to avoid polluting shared staging. Approve?",
    "Documentation and naming: user-centric Given/When/Then-style test names, tests colocated with code, and a lightweight “Test Notes” doc listing critical E2E flows and test users/seeds. OK?"
  ],
  "summary": "Established so far: PoC (Mar 2026) then MVP (May 2026). Key risks: upload/playback reliability, timecode accuracy, completion lock behavior. Single engineer; ad-hoc releases. Test levels for PoC: FE/BE unit, functional, integration (API+Postgres via Testcontainers), and Playwright E2E (Chromium-only). S3 is real in CI; E2E uses real login with Liquibase-seeded users. Coverage targets approved (FE 75/60; BE 80/70). Integration/E2E must cover upload, timecoded comment seek, non-latest read-only with forced-latest commenting, and reversible completion lock. Tooling confirmed (Vitest/RTL/MSW; JUnit5/Spring Boot Test/Mockito/Testcontainers; JaCoCo; Playwright). Quality gates: PR requires lint, typecheck, unit + integration with thresholds; E2E runs on merges to main; pre-deploy smoke (Liquibase validate/migrate, S3 read/write, core flows); security scanning deferred to MVP. Now confirming testing principles: development approach, flaky test policy, E2E data isolation/cleanup, and lightweight test documentation/naming."
}

questions: Development approach: adopt test-first for unit/integration on risky logic, with test-after acceptable for UI wiring. Approve?
Flaky tests policy: allow 1 retry for E2E; if still failing, fail the pipeline. Quarantine only with an issue opened and fix within 48 hours. Approve?
E2E data isolation: suite creates its own project/entities, uses a unique S3 key prefix per run, and cleans up at end to avoid polluting shared staging. Approve?
Documentation and naming: user-centric Given/When/Then-style test names, tests colocated with code, and a lightweight “Test Notes” doc listing critical E2E flows and test users/seeds. OK?
structuredResponse.summary: Established so far: PoC (Mar 2026) then MVP (May 2026). Key risks: upload/playback reliability, timecode accuracy, completion lock behavior. Single engineer; ad-hoc releases. Test levels for PoC: FE/BE unit, functional, integration (API+Postgres via Testcontainers), and Playwright E2E (Chromium-only). S3 is real in CI; E2E uses real login with Liquibase-seeded users. Coverage targets approved (FE 75/60; BE 80/70). Integration/E2E must cover upload, timecoded comment seek, non-latest read-only with forced-latest commenting, and reversible completion lock. Tooling confirmed (Vitest/RTL/MSW; JUnit5/Spring Boot Test/Mockito/Testcontainers; JaCoCo; Playwright). Quality gates: PR requires lint, typecheck, unit + integration with thresholds; E2E runs on merges to main; pre-deploy smoke (Liquibase validate/migrate, S3 read/write, core flows); security scanning deferred to MVP. Now confirming testing principles: development approach, flaky test policy, E2E data isolation/cleanup, and lightweight test documentation/naming.

messages.content:
1. Q: Development approach: adopt test-first for unit/integration on risky logic, with test-after acceptable for UI wiring. Approve? A: test-after only
2. Q: Flaky tests policy: allow 1 retry for E2E; if still failing, fail the pipeline. Quarantine only with an issue opened and fix within 48 hours. Approve? A: Approve
3. Q: E2E data isolation: suite creates its own project/entities, uses a unique S3 key prefix per run, and cleans up at end to avoid polluting shared staging. Approve? A: Approve
4. Q: Documentation and naming: user-centric Given/When/Then-style test names, tests colocated with code, and a lightweight “Test Notes” doc listing critical E2E flows and test users/seeds. OK? A: OK

{
  "phase": "questions",
  "section": "strategy_review",
  "questions": [
    "Confirm E2E target environment: should Playwright on merges to main run against a shared staging environment?",
    "S3 usage by test level: keep real S3 only for E2E and pre-deploy smoke while mocking S3 in unit/integration tests to reduce flakiness/cost, or do you want backend integration tests to also hit real S3?",
    "Post-deployment: include an immediate smoke (health, login, upload, comment seek, complete/uncomplete) with automatic rollback on failure. Approve?",
    "S3 hygiene: add a lifecycle rule to auto-delete test artifacts older than 7 days in addition to test-run cleanup. Approve?"
  ],
  "summary": "Current plan: PoC in Mar 2026 (MVP May 2026). Risks prioritized: upload/playback reliability, timecode accuracy, and completion lock. Single engineer; ad-hoc releases. PoC test levels: FE/BE unit, functional, integration (API+Postgres via Testcontainers), and Playwright E2E (Chromium-only). Real login with Liquibase-seeded users; S3 real bucket available. Coverage thresholds approved: FE unit 75% lines/60% branches; BE unit 80% lines/70% branches. Integration/E2E must cover: upload new version; comment on latest with timecode seek; non-latest read-only with forced-latest commenting; complete lock and reversible uncomplete. Tooling: Vitest/RTL/MSW/c8/ESLint/Prettier/TS; JUnit5/Spring Boot Test/Mockito/Testcontainers/JaCoCo; PIT in MVP. Quality gates: PRs require lint, typecheck, unit+integration with thresholds; self-approval allowed; E2E runs only on merges to main; pre-deploy smoke includes Liquibase validate/migrate, S3 read/write, and core flows; security scanning deferred to MVP. Principles: test-after only; E2E 1 retry then fail with quarantine via issue and 48h fix; E2E data isolation with unique S3 prefix and cleanup; user-centric test naming and lightweight Test Notes. Pending: confirm E2E target environment (staging), finalize S3 use per test level, agree on post-deploy smoke/rollback, and S3 lifecycle cleanup."
}

questions: Confirm E2E target environment: should Playwright on merges to main run against a shared staging environment?

S3 usage by test level: keep real S3 only for E2E and pre-deploy smoke while mocking S3 in unit/integration tests to reduce flakiness/cost, or do you want backend integration tests to also hit real S3?
Post-deployment: include an immediate smoke (health, login, upload, comment seek, complete/uncomplete) with automatic rollback on failure. Approve?
S3 hygiene: add a lifecycle rule to auto-delete test artifacts older than 7 days in addition to test-run cleanup. Approve?
structuredResponse.summary: Current plan: PoC in Mar 2026 (MVP May 2026). Risks prioritized: upload/playback reliability, timecode accuracy, and completion lock. Single engineer; ad-hoc releases. PoC test levels: FE/BE unit, functional, integration (API+Postgres via Testcontainers), and Playwright E2E (Chromium-only). Real login with Liquibase-seeded users; S3 real bucket available. Coverage thresholds approved: FE unit 75% lines/60% branches; BE unit 80% lines/70% branches. Integration/E2E must cover: upload new version; comment on latest with timecode seek; non-latest read-only with forced-latest commenting; complete lock and reversible uncomplete. Tooling: Vitest/RTL/MSW/c8/ESLint/Prettier/TS; JUnit5/Spring Boot Test/Mockito/Testcontainers/JaCoCo; PIT in MVP. Quality gates: PRs require lint, typecheck, unit+integration with thresholds; self-approval allowed; E2E runs only on merges to main; pre-deploy smoke includes Liquibase validate/migrate, S3 read/write, and core flows; security scanning deferred to MVP. Principles: test-after only; E2E 1 retry then fail with quarantine via issue and 48h fix; E2E data isolation with unique S3 prefix and cleanup; user-centric test naming and lightweight Test Notes. Pending: confirm E2E target environment (staging), finalize S3 use per test level, agree on post-deploy smoke/rollback, and S3 lifecycle cleanup.

messages.content:
1. Q: Confirm E2E target environment: should Playwright on merges to main run against a shared staging environment? A: Confirmed
2. Q: S3 usage by test level: keep real S3 only for E2E and pre-deploy smoke while mocking S3 in unit/integration tests to reduce flakiness/cost, or do you want backend integration tests to also hit real S3? A: backend integration tests doesn't need real S3
3. Q: Post-deployment: include an immediate smoke (health, login, upload, comment seek, complete/uncomplete) with automatic rollback on failure. Approve? A: Approve
4. Q: S3 hygiene: add a lifecycle rule to auto-delete test artifacts older than 7 days in addition to test-run cleanup. Approve? A: Approve

structuredResponse.summary: Recap: Scope/timeline PoC Mar 2026, MVP May 2026. Key risks: video upload/playback reliability, timecode accuracy, and completion lock behavior. Team: single engineer; ad-hoc releases. Test levels for PoC: (1) Frontend unit/functional with Vitest + React Testing Library + MSW, c8 for coverage (targets FE unit ≥75% lines/≥60% branches); (2) Backend unit with JUnit 5 + Mockito, JaCoCo (targets BE unit ≥80% lines/≥70% branches); (3) Backend integration with Spring Boot Test + Testcontainers (PostgreSQL), S3 mocked; exercise all DB-related repositories/services with happy path plus at least one failure path each; (4) End-to-end with Playwright (Chromium-only), running on merges to main against shared staging; real login using Liquibase-seeded users; critical flows covered: upload new version; comment on latest and clicking timecode seeks; viewing non-latest prevents new comments and forces comments to latest; producer marks complete (locks comments/uploads) and can reverse. Deferred to MVP: mutation testing (Backend PIT ≥60%; Frontend Stryker optional), light-touch performance/security/accessibility checks, dependency/secret scanning. Tooling/process: ESLint + Prettier + TypeScript checks via pre-commit (Husky/lint-staged); JaCoCo in CI; Playwright traces/screenshots on failure. S3: real bucket for E2E and pre-deploy smoke only; backend integration tests mock S3; unique S3 key prefix per run, test cleanup, plus 7-day lifecycle auto-delete. Quality gates: PRs require lint, typecheck, FE/BE unit tests meeting thresholds, and backend integration tests; self-approval allowed but PR required. CI: E2E runs only on merges to main against staging. Pre-deployment: Liquibase validate/migrate, S3 write/read smoke, and brief smoke of core flows; defined rollback plan (DB backup + app rollback). Post-deployment: immediate smoke (health, login, upload, comment seek, complete/uncomplete) with automatic rollback on failure. Testing principles: test-after development; flaky E2E allowed 1 retry then fail, quarantine with issue and 48h fix window; E2E data isolation (own entities, unique S3 prefix, cleanup); user-centric Given/When/Then naming, tests colocated with code, lightweight Test Notes documenting critical E2E flows and seeded users. Please confirm to save this test strategy.
